text_fr: of  11  Dcccmber  1946, which initiated the fulfil› ment of Article  13,  paragraph 1, sub-paragraph a,  of  the Charter, regarding the development of international law and its codification;
text_en: of 11 Dcccmber 1946, which initiated the fulfil› ment of Article 13, paragraph 1, sub-paragraph a, of the Charter, looking at the development of international law and its codification;

text_fr: Considering that  one  of  the  most  effective means  of  furthering  the  development  of  inter› national law consists in promoting public interest in this subject and using  the media of education and publicity to familiarize  the peoples with the principles  and  rules  that  govern  international relations;
text_en: Considering that one of the most effective means of furthering the development of inter› national law consist in promoting public interest in this subject and using the media of education and publicity to familiarize the people with the principals and rules that govern international relations;

text_fr: Considering that  greater  knowledge  of  and fuller information on the aims, purposes and struc› ture  of  the  United  Nations  constitute  another positive  method of  assisting  the development of international law, of which the United Nations is the main instrument,
text_en: Considering that greater knowledge of and fuller information on theims, purposes and structure› ture of the United Nations constitutes another positive method of assisting the development of international law, of which the United Nations is the main instrument,

text_fr: The General Assembly
text_en: The General Assembly

text_fr: Resolves to  request the Governments of Mem› ber States:
text_en: Resolves to request the Governments of Mem› ber States:

items_fr: 1. To take appropriate measures to extend the teaching of  international law in all its phases, in› cluding its  development and codification,  in the universities and higher educational institutions of each country that are under government control or over which Governments have some influence, or to  initiatc  such  teaching where  it  is  not  yet provided;

2.  To promote similar teaching regarding the aims,  purposes,  structure  and  operation  of  the United Nations in conjunction with paragraph 1 above and in accordance with resolution 137 (II) adopted by the General Assembly on 17  Novem› ber  194 7, on  the  teaching  of  the  purposes and principles, the structure and activities of the United Nations in the schools of Member States;

3.  T o give to the Secretary-General the fullest possible  co-operation  with  a  view  to  facilitating the preparatory work on the development of inter› national law and its  codification  and to support any  individual  or  private  effort  to  these  ends undertaken in their countries.
items_en: 1. To take appropriate measures to extend the teaching of international law in all its phases, in› defining its development and codification, in the universities and higher educational institutions of each country that are under government control or over which Governments have some influence, or to initiate such teaching where it is not yet provided;

2. To promote similar teaching looking at the images, purposes, structure and operation of the United Nations in conjunction with paragraph 1 above and in accordance with resolution 137 (II) adopted by the General Assembly on 17 November› ber 194 7, on the teaching of the purposes and principles, the structure and activities of the United Nations in the schools of Member States;

3. T o give to the Secretary-General the full possible co-operation with a view to facilitating the preparatory work on the development of inter› national law and its codification and to support any individual or private effort to these end undertaken in their countries.

text_fr: Hundred and twenty-third plenary meeting, 21  November 1947.
text_en: Hundred and twenty-third plenary meeting, 21 November 1947.

text_fr: 177  UI).  Formulation of the principles  rec› ognized  in  the  Charter  of  the  Nürn› berg Tribunal and in the judgment of the  Tribunal
text_en: 177 IU). Formulation of the principals rec› acquired in the Charter of the Nürn› berg Tribunal and in the judgment of the Tribunal

text_fr: The General Assembly
text_en: The General Assembly

text_fr: Decides to entrust the formulation of the prin› ciples of international law recognized in the char› ter of the Nürnberg Tribunal and in the judgment of  the Tribunal to the International Law Com› mission, the members of which will, in accordance with  resolution  174 (II), be  elected  at the  next session  of  the General Assembly,  and
text_en: Decisions to entrust the formulation of the prin› ciples of international law recognized in the char› ter of the Nürnberg Tribunal and in the judgment of the Tribunal to the International Law Com› mission, the members of which will, in accordance with resolution 174 (II), be elected at the next session of the General Assembly, and

text_fr: 1946  de  l'Assemblée générale qui a  inauguré la mise en application de l' Article 13, paragraphe 1, alinéa a, de la Charte, relatif au développement progressif  du droit international et à sa codifica› tion;
text_en: 1946 of the General Assembly which inaugurated the implementation of Article 13(1)(a) of the Charter concerning the progressive development of international law and its codification;

text_fr: Considérant que l'une des  façons les  plus effi› caces  de  travailler  au  développement du  droit international  consiste  à  favoriser  l'intérêt du public à son  égard et  à  employer  les  méthodes d'éducation et de propagande tendant à familiari› ser  les  peuples  avec les  principes et les  règles qui régissent les  relations internationales;
text_en: Considering that one of the most effective ways of working towards the development of international law is to promote the public interest in it and to use methods of education and propaganda aimed at the familiari of peoples with the principles and rules that govern international relations;

text_fr: Considérant qu'une connaissance  plus  appro› fondie des buts, des objectifs et de la structure de l'Organisation des  Nations  Unies,  ainsi  qu'une documentation  plus  riche  sur  ces  mêmes sujets, constituent d'autres moyens efficaces de coopérer au développement du droit international dont cet organisme est le principal instrument,
text_en: Considering that a more appropriate knowledge of the purposes, objectives and structure of the United Nations, as well as richer documentation on the same subjects, constitute other effective means of cooperation in the development of international law, of which that body is the principal instrument,

text_fr: L'Assemblée générale
text_en: The General Assembly

text_fr: Décide d'inviter les  Gouvernements  des  Etats Membres:
text_en: Decides to invite the Governments of the Member States:

items_fr: 1.  A  prendre les  mesures propres à intensifier l'enseignement du  droit  international  considéré dans toutes les  phases de son développement et sa codification, dans les  universités et établissements d'enseignement  supérieur de  chaque  pays  qui dépendent du  Gouvernement  ou  dans  lesquels celui-ci peut exercer son influence, ou à organiser cet enseignement dans les  cas oø il  n'existe pas;

2. A  favoriser  de  même l'enseignement relatif aux buts, aux objets, à la structure et au fonction› nement  de  l'Organisation  des  Nations  Unies, compte tenu de ce qui est dit ci-dessus au para› graphe  1,  et  conformément  à  la  résolution 13 7 (II)  adoptée par l'Assemblée générale le  1 7 novembre 1947, relative à l'enseignement des buts et des  principes, de la structure et de l'activité de l'Organisation des  Nations Unies dans les  écoles des  Etats Membres;

3.  A  prêter au Secrétaire général tout le  con› cours  en  leur  pouvoir  pour faciliter  les  travaux de préparation à la codification du droit interna› tional et à son développement, et d'accorder leur appui à toute  initiative  particulière  ou  privée prise dans leurs pays respectifs et tendant aux fins susdites.
items_en: 1. To take appropriate measures to intensify the teaching of relevant international law at all stages of its development and codification, at universities and higher education institutions in each country that is dependent on or in which the Government may exercise its influence, or to organize such teaching in cases where there is no such education;

2. To promote the teaching of the purposes, objects, structure and function of the United Nations, taking into account the above mentioned in paragraph 1 above, and in accordance with General Assembly resolution 13 7 (II) of 1 7 November 1947 on the teaching of the purposes and principles, structure and activities of the United Nations in schools of Member States;

3. To give the Secretary-General all the assistance in their power to facilitate work in preparation for the codification of international law and its development, and to give their support to any particular or private initiative taken in their respective countries for the purposes set out above.

text_fr: Cent-vingt-troisième séance plénière, le21 novembre 1947.
text_en: One-hundred-and-twenty-third plenary meeting, on 21 November 1947.

text_fr: 177 (Il). Formulation  des  principes  recon› nus par le Statut de la Cour de Nurem› berg et dans l'arrêt de cette  Cour
text_en: 177 (Il). Formulation of the principles recognized by the Statute of the Court of Nurem› berg and in the judgment of that Court

text_fr: L'Assemblée générale
text_en: The General Assembly

text_fr: Décide de confier la formulation des  principes de droit international reconnus par le statut de la Cour de Nuremberg et dans l'arrêt de cette Cour, à-la Commission du droit international, dont les membres  seront,  conformément à la  résolution 17 4 (II), élus à la prochaine session del' Assemblée ,  générale, et
text_en: Decides to entrust the formulation of the principles of international law recognized in the Statute of the Court of Nuremberg and in the Judgment of that Court to the International Law Commission, whose members shall be elected, in accordance with resolution 17 4 (II), at the next session of the General Assembly, and

text_fr: Directs the Commission to
text_en: Directs the Commission to

items_fr: (a) Formulate the  principles  of  international law  recognized  in  the  Charter of  the  Nürnberg Tribunal and in  the judgment of the Tribunal, and
( b) Prepare a  draft code  of  offences  against the  peace  and  security  of  mankind,  indicating clearly the place to be accorded to the principks mentioned  in  sub-paragraph (a) above.
items_en: (a) Formulate the principals of international law recognized in the Charter of the Nürnberg Tribunal and in the judgment of the Tribunal, and
(b) Prepare a draft code of offers against the peace and security of mankind, indicating cleanly the place to be agreed to the principals mentioned in sub-paragraph (a) above.

text_fr: l/U,ndred and twenty-third plenary meeting, 21  November 1947.
text_en: l/U,ndred and twenty-third plenary meeting, 21 November 1947.

text_fr: 178 (Il). Draft  declaration  on  the  rights and duties of States
text_en: 178 (Il). Draft declaration on the rights and duties of States

text_fr: The General Assembly,
text_en: The General Assembly,

text_fr: Noting that very  few  comments and observa› tions  on the draft declaration  on  the  rights  and duties of States presented by Panama 1 have been received from  the States Members of the  United Nations,
text_en: Noting that very many comments and observations on the draft declaration on the rights and duties of States presented by Panama 1 have been received from the States Members of the United Nations,

text_fr: Requests the Secretary-General to draw the at› tention of States to  the desirability of submitting their comments and ob~crvations  without delay;
text_en: Requests the Secretary-General to draw the at› custody of States to the desire of submitting their comments and ob~crvations without delay;

text_fr: Requests the  Secretary-Gcneral  to  undertake the  neccssary  preparatory  work  on  the  draft declaration on the rights and duties of States ac› cording to the terms of resolution  17 S (II) ;
text_en: Requests the Secretary-Gcneral to undertake the successful preparation work on the draft declaration on the rights and duties of States ac› cording to the terms of resolution 17 S (II);

text_fr: Resolves to  entrust further study of this  prob› lem  to  the  International  Law  Commission,  the members of which in accordancc with the terms of resolution  174( II)  will  be elccted  at the next session of the Gencral Asscmbly,
text_en: Resolves to entrust further study of this prob› lem to the International Law Commission, the members of which in accordance with the terms of resolution 174( II) will be elccted at the next session of the Gencral Asscmbly,

text_fr: And accordingly
text_en: And accordingly

text_fr: Instructs the International Law Commission to prcpare  a  draft  dcclaration  on  the  rights  and duties of States, taking as a basis of discussion the draft declaration on the rights and duties of States presented by Panama, and taking into considera› tion  other documents and drafts on this subject.
text_en: Instructs the International Law Commission to prepare a draft declaration on the rights and duties of States, taking as a basis of discussion the draft declaration on the rights and duties of States presented by Panama, and taking into consideration›tion other documents and drafts on this subject.

text_en: 179 (Il}. Co-ordination of the privileges and immunities of the United Nation and of the specialized agencies

text_fr: Cent-vingt-troisième séance plénière, le  21  novembre 1947.
text_en: One-hundred-and-twenty-third plenary meeting, on 21 November 1947.

text_fr: l 78  (Il).  Projet  de déclaration des droits  et des devoirs des Etats
text_en: l 78 (I). Draft declaration of the rights and duties of States

text_fr: L'Assemblée générale,
text_en: The General Assembly,

text_fr: Prenant note du fait qu'un nombre restreint de commentaires  et  d'observations sur le  projet  de déclaration des  droits  et  des  devoirs  des  Etats présenté par  le  Panama' a  été reçu  des  Etats :\lcmbrcs de l'Organisation des  Natiôns  Unies,
text_en: Taking note of the fact that a limited number of comments and observations on the draft declaration of the rights and duties of States submitted by Panama have been received from States:\lmbrcs of the United Nations Organization,

text_fr: Invite le  Secrétaire général à attirer l'attention des  Etats  sur  l'intérêt qu'il y  a à ce  que  leurs commentaires et  observations soient  fournis  sans délai;
text_en: Invites the Secretary-General to draw the attention of States to the value of their comments and observations being provided without delay;

text_fr: Invite le  Secrétaire général à entreprendre  le travail préparatoire nécessaire en cc qui concerne le  projet de  déclaration des  droits  et des  devoirs des  Etats  conformément aux  dispositions  de  la résolution l 7 S (II) ;
text_en: Invites the Secretary-General to undertake the necessary preparatory work on the draft declaration of the rights and duties of States in accordance with the provisions of resolution l 7 S (II);

text_fr: Décide de  confier  les  études ultérieures con› cernant cette matière à la  Commission  du droit international dont les  membres seront, conformé› ment  aux dispositions  de  la  résolution t 74 (II), élus à la  prochaine session  de  l'Assemblée géné› rale;
text_en: Decides to entrust subsequent studies on this subject to the International Law Commission, whose members shall be elected at the next session of the General Assembly, in accordance with the provisions of resolution T 74 (II);

text_fr: En conséquence,
text_en: As a result,

text_fr: Charge la Commission du droit international de préparer un projet de déclaration des droits et des devoirs des  Etats, en prenant comme base de dis› cussion  le  projet de déclaration des  droits et  des devoirs  des  Etat~  présenté par le  Panama et  en tenant  compte  des  autres  documents  et  projets relatifs à ce sujet.
text_en: Requests the International Law Commission to prepare a draft declaration of the rights and duties of States, taking as a basis for discussion the draft declaration of the rights and duties of States submitted by Panama and taking into account other documents and drafts relating to this subject.

text_fr: ,1 f,pro111·c la Convention  suivante  sur  les privilèges et immunités des institutions spécialisées et la propose à l'acceptation des  institutions spé› cialisées et à l'adhésion de tous les  Etats Membres de  l'Organisation des  Nations  Unies  et  de  tout autre Etat membre d'une institution spécialisée.
text_en: The following Convention on the Privileges and Immunities of the Specialized Agencies is proposed for acceptance by the specialized institutions and for accession by all Member States of the United Nations and any other Member State of a specialized agency.
